text.title: discriminated-union-output Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for discriminated-union-output as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Declaring action: SearchAction | FetchAction | FinishAction without Field(discriminator="kind").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pydantic falls back to smart union mode and the JSON schema emits anyOf without a discriminator keyword. OpenAI strict mode then has to try every branch; with overlapping fields this either fails to compile or accepts cross-branch data.

failure-mode.description: Defining a branch as class SearchAction(BaseModel): query: str; kind: Literal["search"] = "search" — discriminator at the bottom.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Order matters in autoregressive generation. With the literal at the end, the model must commit to query before it has chosen the branch, which defeats the grammar-locking benefit. Always declare the discriminator first.

failure-mode.description: Modelling EmailMessage vs SmsMessage as a discriminated union when both share recipient, body, sent_at, and only differ by one channel-specific field.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Two near-identical branches double the schema size, double the prompt-cache footprint, and force the model to learn two shapes for one concept. Use a single model with a channel: Literal["email","sms"] discriminator and channel-specific optional fields instead.
